query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "agentic abstention: do agents know when to stop instead of act?" computer science
rationale: The paper investigates a fundamental limitation of LLMs regarding character-level understanding, which is a distinct construct from the user's question about "agentic abstention" (the mechanism of an agent deciding when to stop acting). While both topics concern LLM capabilities, the paper does not measure the same mechanism, variables, or empirical setting related to agent decision-making or stopping criteria, and thus fails to meet any of the inclusion criteria (a-f).
